feed.title: Unfiltered Soccer with Landon Donovan & Tim Howard (filtered)

entry.title: [UFS] Carlos Bocanegra on USMNT's World Cup, Leadership Issues & Fixing Youth Soccer | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
USMNT legend Carlos Bocanegra joined former teammates Landon Donovan and Tim Howard for an in-depth conversation about the United States' 2026 FIFA World Cup run and the future of American soccer.
Carlos shares why he was initially skeptical of the expanded World Cup, why he believes the criticism of the USMNT was unfair, and which players impressed him the most, including Malik Tillman, Weston McKennie, Folarin Balogun, and Alex Freeman.
The trio also discusses leadership within the current squad, Mauricio Pochettino's future, what American players need to do to become stars at Europe's biggest clubs, and how youth soccer in the United States must evolve to compete with the world's elite. Bocanegra also explains why creating the right environment for young talent is critical.
#USMNT #CarlosBocanegra #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #WorldCup #Soccer #Football #Pochettino

USMNT legend Carlos Bocanegra joined former teammates Landon Donovan and Tim Howard for an in-depth conversation about the United States' 2026 FIFA World Cup run and the future of American soccer.

Carlos shares why he was initially skeptical of the expanded World Cup, why he believes the criticism of the USMNT was unfair, and which players impressed him the most, including Malik Tillman, Weston McKennie, Folarin Balogun, and Alex Freeman.

The trio also discusses leadership within the current squad, Mauricio Pochettino's future, what American players need to do to become stars at Europe's biggest clubs, and how youth soccer in the United States must evolve to compete with the world's elite. Bocanegra also explains why creating the right environment for young talent is critical.

entry.title: [UFS] Jason Kelce on Messi, USMNT World Cup Hopes & Why Soccer Is Harder Than Football | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Jason Kelce is getting Unfiltered! Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are joined by former Philadelphia Eagles center and podcast host Jason Kelce to discuss his love of soccer. The guys talk about the US Men’s National Team’s World Cup run so far, why USA soccer chants are SO BAD, Messi still being the GOAT at 39, and why youth soccer in America is due for a change.
New episodes of Unfiltered Soccer with Landon and Tim drop every Wednesday & Saturday during this summer’s tournament. Subscribe to the show on YouTube and follow on all your favorite podcast platforms. For bonus content, follow on all social media platforms @UnfilteredSoccer. entry.title: [UFS] Messi and Ronaldo Are DEFYING Age at the World Cup 😱 | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Two USMNT legends are in awe of what they're witnessing from football's greatest rivals.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss the incredible World Cup performances of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo as both stars continue to rewrite soccer history.
Messi and Ronaldo have become the first and second men's players ever to appear on the pitch in six FIFA World Cups. Ronaldo added another historic chapter by scoring twice against Uzbekistan, while Messi celebrated his 39th birthday during the tournament and has already scored five goals in just two World Cup matches.
Howard and Donovan compare the longevity and dominance of Messi and Ronaldo to legendary late-career performances from athletes across other sports, asking a fascinating question:
Are we witnessing the greatest aging athletes in sports history?
Watch as the USMNT icons break down what makes Messi and Ronaldo's sustained excellence so remarkable and why their World Cup performances continue to amaze even former elite players.
⚽ Do you think Messi or Ronaldo's longevity is more impressive?

entry.title: [UFS] Tim Howard Rips Red Cards and DEFENDS Jesse Marsch 🤯 | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
The first red card of the World Cup for mouth-covering has everyone talking after Paraguay's Miguel Almirón was sent off against Tükiye. USMNT legends Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down the controversial decision and they don't agree.
Landon questions the player's actions, while Tim argues the red card was excessive, even calling many yellow and red cards "dumb." The discussion then shifts to Jesse Marsch, one of soccer's most polarizing coaches. Tim comes to Marsch's defense, explaining why he believes the Canada manager's actions and personality are irrelevant and that it's the results that matter.
Do you agree with Tim or Landon? Should Almirón have been sent off? And what's your take on Jesse Marsch?
👇 Sound off in the comments!
#USMNT #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #MiguelAlmiron #Paraguay #JesseMarsch #CanadaSoccer #WorldCup #SoccerNews #FootballNews #SoccerDebate #WorldCup2026 #FIFA

entry.title: [UFS] Messi Dominates While Lamine Yamal Makes His World Cup Debut 👀
entry.summary:
Lionel Messi continues to prove why he's one of the greatest players in soccer history, while Lamine Yamal officially announced himself on the World Cup stage. 🌎⚽
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down Argentina and Spain's opening matches at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlighting Messi's brilliance and discussing what made Yamal's highly anticipated World Cup debut so special.
From the GOAT's continued excellence to the rise of one of soccer's brightest young stars, this episode covers two of the biggest storylines of the tournament so far.
Can Messi lead Argentina to another World Cup title? And how far can Lamine Yamal take Spain?

entry.title: [UFS] USMNT's Biggest Question Before Australia: Will Christian Pulisic Play? | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Christian Pulisic's status could have a major impact on the USMNT's biggest World Cup match yet.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down the latest on Pulisic's availability and preview a crucial showdown between the United States and Australia at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Can the USMNT continue its strong start, or will the Socceroos provide their toughest test of the tournament so far? The guys discuss key matchups, expectations, and what the U.S. needs to do to secure another massive result.

entry.title: [UFS] Landon Donovan & Tim Howard Reveal the Right Way to Approach Soccer Legends | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
What's the right way to approach Landon Donovan and Tim Howard?
USMNT legends Landon Donovan and Tim Howard share their thoughts on being recognized by fans, including the best way to approach them and whether it's okay to "bro them up."
The conversation also turns to the FIFA World Cup, as Donovan and Howard offer advice for casual fans looking to get the most out of the tournament experience.
From fan interactions to World Cup culture, this episode is packed with stories and insights from two of the biggest names in U.S. Soccer.

entry.title: [UFS] What Happens If the USMNT Loses Pochettino? | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
What does U.S. Soccer look for in a head coach?
With reports linking USMNT head coach Mauricio Pochettino to AC Milan after the World Cup, questions about the future of the position continue to surface. U.S. Soccer CEO and Secretary General JT Batson joins Landon Donovan and Tim Howard to discuss what the federation values in a head coach and how leadership shapes the direction of the program.
The conversation also explores the evolution of soccer in the United States and where the sport is headed in the years ahead.

entry.title: [UFS] The 2026 USMNT World Cup Preview | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
The road to the 2026 World Cup starts here.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard preview the 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America and break down where the USMNT stands as the team wraps up its pre-tournament friendlies.
The guys are also joined by U.S. Soccer CEO and Secretary General JT Batson for a conversation about the tournament, the future of the sport in America, and what comes next after the World Cup. Plus, in the AT&T Mailbag, Landon and Tim discuss fan interactions and share what supporters should know as World Cup action gets underway.

entry.title: [UFS] Christian Pulisic's Defining World Cup Moment vs Iran | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Reliving one of the biggest goals in modern USMNT history.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard look back on Christian Pulisic's decisive goal against Iran at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.
With the United States needing a win to advance after draws against Wales and England, the pressure could not have been higher. Donovan and Howard revisit the match, the atmosphere, and the moment that sent the USMNT into the knockout stage.
The episode also features a look back at the road to that match and a trivia challenge centered around one of the most memorable nights in recent U.S. Soccer history.

entry.title: [UFS] Matt Freese or Matt Turner: Who Should Start for the USMNT? | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Who should be the USMNT's No. 1 goalkeeper at the World Cup?
The competition for the United States' starting goalkeeper spot remains one of the biggest storylines heading into the FIFA World Cup. With Matt Freese and Matt Turner both in the conversation, Tim Howard and Landon Donovan break down the goalkeeper rotation and what it could mean for Mauricio Pochettino's squad.
The discussion also covers Christian Pulisic ending his goalless streak, the USMNT's performance against Senegal, and the key takeaways as the team prepares for the tournament. From selection decisions to standout performances, Donovan and Howard analyze where the USMNT stands heading into the World Cup.

entry.title: [UFS] Pulisic Scores, USMNT Goalkeeper Debate & Arsenal's Champions League Collapse | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
The World Cup is about to get Unfiltered.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down the USMNT's match against Senegal, including Christian Pulisic ending his goal drought and the ongoing competition for the United States' No. 1 goalkeeper spot.
The conversation also turns to Mauricio Pochettino, recent reports surrounding his future, and what it could mean for the USMNT heading into the FIFA World Cup.
Away from international soccer, the guys discuss PSG's Champions League victory over Arsenal and debate where Arsenal stand among Europe's elite clubs.
Plus, in the AT&T Mailbag, Landon and Tim answer questions about jersey numbers and Diego Luna being left off the USMNT World Cup roster.

entry.title: [UFS] The Night Tim Howard Made World Cup History | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Reliving one of the greatest performances in World Cup history.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard revisit one of the most memorable matches in U.S. Soccer history: the United States' Round of 16 showdown with Belgium at the 2014 World Cup.
After advancing from a difficult group, the USMNT faced a Belgian side loaded with talent. Over the course of the match, Tim Howard delivered a record-setting performance between the posts, making more saves than any goalkeeper had ever recorded in a World Cup match.
Donovan and Howard look back on the match, the atmosphere, the challenges posed by Belgium, and the performance that cemented Howard's place in World Cup history.

entry.title: [UFS] Clint Dempsey & Bob Bradley Get Candid on U.S. Soccer | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Clint Dempsey reflects on his rise with the USMNT.
In a candid conversation with former teammates Landon Donovan and Tim Howard, Clint Dempsey looks back on his journey with the United States Men's National Team and some of the defining moments of his international career.
The discussion includes Dempsey's experience during the 2009 FIFA Confederations Cup and a memorable conversation with former USMNT head coach Bob Bradley, offering insight into a pivotal period for the program.
From leadership and competition to the growth of American soccer, the episode revisits one of the most influential eras in USMNT history.

entry.title: [UFS] Landon Donovan's Iconic Goal vs Algeria | The Greatest Moment in USMNT History? | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Reliving one of the most iconic moments in USMNT history.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard revisit the unforgettable match between the United States and Algeria at the 2010 World Cup — a game remembered for Donovan's dramatic stoppage-time winner that sent the USMNT into the knockout stage.
The two U.S. Soccer legends break down the play from start to finish, reflecting on the buildup, the outlet pass, the finish, and the emotions surrounding one of the most celebrated goals in American soccer history.
From the tension of the moment to the aftermath of the goal, Donovan and Howard provide a firsthand look at a defining chapter in USMNT lore.

entry.title: [UFS] Clint Dempsey: “Bury Me With The National Team Jersey” | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Clint Dempsey gets Unfiltered.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are joined by fellow USMNT legend Clint Dempsey for a wide-ranging conversation covering his career, his upbringing in Texas, and some of his most memorable moments representing the United States.
Dempsey reflects on World Cup experiences alongside teammates such as Landon Donovan and DaMarcus Beasley, discusses former USMNT head coach Bob Bradley, and shares his perspective on what it means to wear the national team jersey.
The conversation also includes Dempsey's thoughts on which club Hall of Fame he would most want to be inducted into, the greatest player he ever played with, and the former teammates who would make his ultimate 5-a-side team.

entry.title: [UFS] Yunus Musah's Costly Mistake + Moyes vs. Klinsmann | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
What happened to Yunus Musah's World Cup hopes?
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss Yunus Musah's position ahead of the FIFA World Cup and share their perspectives on the factors that have shaped his path with the USMNT.
The conversation also provides insight into how managers communicate lineup decisions, including the process of informing players about the starting XI before a match.
From roster battles to team management, Donovan and Howard break down another key storyline surrounding the United States as the World Cup approaches.

entry.title: [UFS] Chris Richards Injury Concern + Seamus Coleman's Everton Exit | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Chris Richards and Seamus Coleman headline this week's soccer discussion.
With Chris Richards dealing with an ankle injury ahead of the FIFA World Cup, questions remain about what the setback could mean for the USMNT defender and his preparations for the tournament. The injury also puts his availability for Crystal Palace's Europa Conference League Final into focus.
The conversation also turns to Everton, where longtime captain Seamus Coleman has announced that his 17-year run with the club will come to an end after the season.
From World Cup storylines to one of the Premier League's most respected careers, the episode covers two major developments in the soccer world.

entry.summary:
Major changes and major questions across the Premier League.
Liverpool are back in the headlines as Mohamed Salah publicly shared his frustrations with the current state of the club, adding another chapter to one of the biggest ongoing stories in English soccer.
Elsewhere, Manchester United have made a decision on their future by keeping Michael Carrick as the club's permanent head coach, while Chelsea have turned to Xabi Alonso to lead the next era at Stamford Bridge.
From managerial moves to one of the Premier League's biggest stars making headlines, this episode breaks down some of the most significant developments in European soccer.

entry.title: [UFS] USMNT's Revenge on Ghana | Reliving the Iconic 2014 World Cup Victory | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Reliving the USMNT's long-awaited World Cup redemption.
As the countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup continues, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard revisit one of the most memorable victories in recent U.S. Soccer history — the United States' dramatic win over Ghana at the 2014 World Cup.
After World Cup eliminations at the hands of Ghana in both 2006 and 2010, the USMNT faced a familiar opponent once again in its opening match in Brazil. This time, however, the outcome was different.
Donovan and Howard look back on Clint Dempsey's early goal, John Brooks' dramatic game-winner, and the emotions surrounding a match that remains one of the defining moments of the 2014 tournament for the United States.
#USMNT #WorldCup2014 #Ghana #ClintDempsey #JohnBrooks #LandonDonovan #TimHoward #USSoccer #AmericanSoccer #WorldCup #SoccerHistory #USMNTFans #SoccerTalk #WorldCupMoments #RoadTo2026

entry.title: [UFS] Jozy Altidore's USMNT Exit, Chris Richards Injury & Yunus Musah Questions | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Jozy Altidore gets Unfiltered.
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard are joined by former USMNT teammate Jozy Altidore for a candid conversation covering some of the biggest moments and turning points of his career.
Altidore discusses the aftermath of the United States' failure to qualify for the 2018 FIFA World Cup, his experience playing for Sunderland, and the future of Yunus Musah within the U.S. Soccer program.
The episode also examines Chris Richards' injury situation ahead of the World Cup and Auston Trusty's role in helping Celtic add another trophy to its collection.
From USMNT storylines to club soccer headlines, Altidore joins Donovan and Howard for an in-depth look at the game.

entry.title: [UFS] Will Pulisic and McKennie Get the USMNT Ending They Deserve? | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Who could be next in line for a legendary USMNT send-off?
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss which current U.S. Men's National Team players are building the kind of careers that could one day earn a memorable farewell, with Christian Pulisic and Weston McKennie among the names featured in the conversation.
The two USMNT legends also debate which World Cup stars from other nations would make the biggest impact if added to the United States squad and what those players could bring to the team. Plus, Donovan and Howard recognize Lionel Messi's latest MLS milestone after the Inter Miami star became the fastest player in league history to reach 100 goal contributions.

entry.content:
Who could be next in line for a legendary USMNT send-off?

Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss which current U.S. Men's National Team players are building the kind of careers that could one day earn a memorable farewell, with Christian Pulisic and Weston McKennie among the names featured in the conversation.

The two USMNT legends also debate which World Cup stars from other nations would make the biggest impact if added to the United States squad and what those players could bring to the team.
Plus, Donovan and Howard recognize Lionel Messi's latest MLS milestone after the Inter Miami star became the fastest player in league history to reach 100 goal contributions.

entry.title: [UFS] Is Arsenal's Title Run Legit? | Unfiltered Soccer | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
VARSENAL? Arsenal, VAR, and Real Madrid drama take center stage.
Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down Arsenal's latest Premier League result, including the controversial VAR decision that overturned a potential West Ham equalizer and helped preserve Arsenal's position at the top of the table.
The conversation also shifts to Spain, where the USMNT legends discuss the latest developments surrounding Real Madrid and one of the biggest storylines in world soccer.
From title races to controversy and club drama, Donovan and Howard weigh in on some of the game's hottest topics.

entry.title: [UFS] Should USMNT Fans Be Worried Before the World Cup? | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
The latest USMNT injury concerns ahead of the World Cup.
With the FIFA World Cup approaching, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard break down several key developments involving United States Men's National Team players competing at the club level. The discussion includes injuries to Christian Pulisic and Johnny Cardoso, what those situations could mean moving forward, and the latest surrounding their availability as the tournament draws closer.
The guys also discuss Gio Reyna finding the scoresheet for Borussia Mönchengladbach and what his recent form could mean for the USMNT.
From injuries and roster implications to performances abroad, Donovan and Howard cover the biggest storylines surrounding the United States ahead of the World Cup.

entry.title: [UFS] Christian Pulisic Injured, Johnny Cardoso Surgery & Gio Reyna's Big Night | Unfiltered Soccer
entry.summary:
Is Johnny Cardoso's World Cup in doubt?
On this episode of Unfiltered Soccer, Landon Donovan and Tim Howard discuss Johnny Cardoso's recent ankle surgery and what it could mean for his chances of making the USMNT's World Cup roster. The conversation also covers Christian Pulisic's injury situation and Gio Reyna getting on the scoresheet for Borussia Mönchengladbach.
The guys also break down Arsenal's win over West Ham, including the controversial decision that disallowed a potential equalizer, before turning their attention to the latest developments surrounding Real Madrid and one of the biggest storylines in European soccer.
Plus, in the AT&T Mailbag, Donovan and Howard discuss former USMNT players who deserved bigger sendoffs, international stars they would have loved to see represent the United States, and what a co-ed all-time U.S. starting XI might look like.
